mnemonics.title: PD-ND-PSD-NSD-IND Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: Think: 'Please Do Not Put Sad Notes Indefinitely' - P=Positive, D=Definite, N=Negative, P=Positive, S=Semidefinite, N=Negative, I=Indefinite. Actually easier: 'All Positive is PD, All Negative is ND, Some Zero is SD, Mix signs is Indefinite'

mnemonics.title: Sylvester's Criterion
mnemonics.content: Leading principal minors must 'Keep Growing Positively' - KGP: Keep Growing Positively for positive definiteness check

acronyms.usage: All eigenvalues ≥ 0, Q(x) ≥ 0 for all x
All eigenvalues ≤ 0, Q(x) ≤ 0 for all x
Mixed eigenvalues: some positive, some negative

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a bowl-shaped surface opening upward - always above zero except at origin
Imagine an inverted bowl - always below zero except at origin
Imagine a saddle surface - goes up in some directions, down in others
Think of taking corner slices of the matrix: top-left 1×1, then 2×2, then 3×3 - all must be positive for PD

analogies.analogy: Like elevation above sea level - always positive except at sea level (origin)
Like a mountain pass - goes up on one side, down on the other side
Like rotating aellipse to align with coordinate axes - the rotation is orthogonal transformation
The symmetric matrix is like a recipe - it tells how x and y ingredients combine to produce the final output
